segments.text: O God, the nations have invaded your inheritance; they have defiled your holy temple, they have reduced Jerusalem to rubble.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: They have left the dead bodies of your servants as food for the birds of the sky, the flesh of your own people for the animals of the wild.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: They have poured out blood like water all around Jerusalem, and there is no one to bury the dead.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a source of mockery and derision to those around us.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: How long, Lord? Will you be angry forever? Will your jealousy burn like fire?
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Pour out your wrath on the nations that do not acknowledge you, on the kingdoms that do not call on your name.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: For they have devoured Jacob and devastated his homeland.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Do not hold against us the sins of past generations; may your mercy come quickly to meet us, for we are in desperate need.
segments.speaker: Asaph

footnotes: former iniquities: or, the iniquities of them that were before us

segments.text: Help us, God our Savior, for the glory of your name; deliver us and forgive our sins for your name's sake.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Why should the nations say, 'Where is their God?' Let there be known among the nations, in our sight, an act of vengeance for the outpoured blood of your servants.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: May the groans of the prisoners come before you; by the strength of your arm preserve those condemned to die.
segments.speaker: Asaph

footnotes: preserve the children of death: Heb. reserve the children of death

segments.text: Repay our neighbors seven times over, Lord, the reproach they have hurled at you.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Then we your people, the sheep of your pasture, will praise you forever; from generation to generation we will proclaim your praise.
segments.speaker: Asaph

footnotes: to all generations: Heb. to generation and generation
